feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Approval Workflow for Manage Automatic Payment
entry.content:
Hi

I would like to know whether there is an approval workflow available for Automatic Payments in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud before the payment document is posted.

I have reviewed and tested the functionality described in the following SAP Learning content:

https://learning.sap.com/videos/workflow-for-payment-approval-sap-s-4hana-cloud-finance

However, based on my testing, the payment document is already posted before the approval workflow is triggered.

My business requirement is to have an approval step before the payment document is posted and the payment is executed.

Could you please advise whether this scenario is supported by standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality? If so, could you provide guidance on the appropriate process or configuration?

entry.title: BADI To maintain Header text of the PO at the backend
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings.

Based on the client requirement, the Purchase Order header text should be maintained in the backend and fetched automatically whenever a Purchase Order is created.

Could you please confirm whether there is any BAdI (Business Add-In) available to implement this requirement? If so, kindly share the details.

Regards,

Shaik.gaffar@in.ey.com

entry.title: How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MI
entry.content:
**Title:** How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MIRO?

**Body:**

We have a customer requirement to hide the "Post" button for general (non-approver) users in the following apps:
- Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)
- Supplier Invoices List (F1060A)
- MIRO

**Business background:**
The intended process is:
1. General user creates a supplier invoice and clicks "Save as Completed"
2. Approver reviews and approves via My Inbox

General users should never be able to post directly. We already have a BAdI implementation (MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) that blocks direct posting with an error message when the invoice status isn't "Parked and completed." However, the customer specifically wants the Post button to be **hidden**, not just blocked with an error.

**What we've tried so far:**

1. **Removing Business Catalogs** SAP_FIN_BC_AA_POST_REG_PC and SAP_FIN_BC_AA_DOC_PROC_PC from the role. This does hide the Post button, but other unrelated apps tied to the same catalogs also lose access as a side effect. We'd like to avoid this catalog-level approach due to that collateral impact.

2. **Using IAM App Authorization Variants** (available since 2502) to control access at a more granular level than the catalog. We found two variants tied to transaction F0859:
- F0859_TRAN ("Create Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and posting selection"
- F0859_77_TRAN ("Hold Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and holding (not posting)"

We deactivated F0859_TRAN while keeping F0859_77_TRAN active, expecting the Post button to disappear while Park/Save as Completed remained available. However, **the Post button is still displayed** after deactivation. Is this the wrong IAM App to target? Is there a different/additional app or restriction type that actually controls Post button visibility?

**Additional question on MIRO:**
Our environment also has the classic MIRO transaction. It doesn't appear when we search the IAM App list for "supplier invoice" apps tied to F0859. Does MIRO's Post authorization come from a completely separate business catalog/IAM app? If so, which one, and does it need to be addressed separately from the Fiori Create Supplier Invoice app?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any guidance on the correct approach (catalog restriction, IAM App Authorization Variant, or something else) — and confirmation on how MIRO fits into this — would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 – Incorrect customer postings after changing customer in Reprocess
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are experiencing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 while processing electronic bank statements using the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app.

Scenario

  1. An electronic bank statement is imported.
  2. The bank statement item is created with Account Type = Customer.
  3. The system automatically proposes Customer A.
  4. The proposed customer is incorrect, so we manually change it to Customer B.
  5. The bank statement item is then posted.

Actual Behavior

Although the customer has been changed to Customer B, the accounting postings generated in the background include entries for both Customer A and Customer B.

The transaction should only relate to Customer B, but debit/credit postings are also created for the originally proposed Customer A.

Expected Behavior

After changing the customer to Customer B, we expect all subsequent processing and accounting postings to be based solely on Customer B. No accounting entries should be created for the originally proposed Customer A.

Questions

  1. How does SAP determine the initial default customer during bank statement processing?
  2. After the customer is changed manually in Reprocess Bank Statement Items, should all subsequent accounting postings be generated only for the updated customer?
  3. Is this standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, or could it indicate a product issue?
  4. Is there any SAP Help documentation or SAP Note that explains this behavior?

Any insights or references would be appreciated.

Thank you.

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, #SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance, #Accounts Receivable

entry.title: **Title:** Does BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD block posting in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859), Suppl
entry.content:
**Title:** Does BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD block posting in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859), Supplier Invoices List (F1060A), and MIRO?

**Body:**

We have a customer requirement to prevent general (non-approver) users from posting supplier invoices directly, bypassing the approval workflow.

**Intended process:**
1. General user creates a supplier invoice and clicks "Save as Completed" (which triggers the approval workflow)
2. Approver reviews and posts the invoice via My Inbox

We understand from SAP KBA 3673139 that hiding the "Post" button is not possible through standard configuration, and that implementing the BAdI **MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD** (in the Custom Fields and Logic app) is the recommended approach to block direct posting with an error message.

**Our question:**
We would like to confirm whether a single implementation of BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD reliably blocks direct posting in **all** of the following apps/transactions. Could you confirm for each one individually?

1. **Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)** — Fiori app
2. **Supplier Invoices List (F1060A)** — Fiori app
3. **MIRO** — Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (MIR4/MIR7-based)

Specifically:
- Does the BAdI's action hook (Post) fire consistently across all three, including the MIRO/Advanced app?
- Is there any app or transaction among these where this BAdI does **not** get triggered, and a different mechanism would be required?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any confirmation based on documentation or real project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: mistaken post
entry.content: I posted this by mistake. Please ignore it. Thank you.

entry.title: Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplie
entry.content:
Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplier invoices in Public Cloud?

**Body:**

I'd like to confirm my understanding of the following matrix for supplier invoice entry in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Could someone with product knowledge confirm whether this is correct, or point out any mistakes?

| App | PO 3-way match | Alternative recon. account | Approval Workflow | Posting control (BAdI/standard) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced / MIRO** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Incoming Invoices (FB60)** — FI | No | Yes | No (not available in Public) | No |

My understanding behind each cell:
- Alternative reconciliation account can only be set in the FI app (FB60), because it is an FI function. In MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO), the reconciliation account field is greyed out.
- BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD only applies to MM Invoice Verification (LIV), so it does not fire for FB60.
- The approval workflow (Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices) works with F0859 / MIRO but is not integrated with FB60 in Public Edition.

**My main question:**
Based on the above, it appears that in Public Edition there is **no standard way to use an alternative reconciliation account while also enforcing posting control via workflow or BAdI** — because the alternative reconciliation account requires FB60 (FI), but posting control (workflow / MRM BAdI) only works with MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO). Is this correct? Is there any standard method, configuration, or supported approach to achieve both at the same time?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any confirmation or correction based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Error: User CB9980000001 is not assigned to any tolerance group for clearing
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I am encountering the following error while posting the Physical Inventory Document:

"User CB9980000001 is not assigned to any tolerance group for clearing."

I have already completed all the required configuration, but the issue still persists.

Please find the attached screenshot/document for your reference and kindly help me resolve this issue.

Looking forward to your guidance.

Regards,

Sudhir Singh

entry.title: **Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in
entry.content:
**Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in MIRO (Msg F5439)

**Body:**

We are configuring the alternative reconciliation account function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It works in the Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) app, but fails in **MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced)** in all cases. I'd appreciate help understanding why.

**Configuration we have done:**

1. **SSCUI 102631 (Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts):** defined the alternative reconciliation accounts allowed for the standard reconciliation account.
2. **SSCUI 103508 (Define Account Group):** since the standard account group ABST cannot be modified, we created a new account group **ZZST** by copying ABST, and set the "reconciliation account ready for input" indicator as optional.
3. **G/L Account Master (Fiori ID F0731A):** assigned account group **ZZST** to both the reconciliation account G/L and the alternative reconciliation account G/L.

**Test results:**

| App | Invoice type | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | G/L account item-based | OK |
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | PO reference-based | OK |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | G/L account item-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | PO reference-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |

**Error message (F5439):**
"Mark the G/L reconciliation account as ready for input" — the message states that to enter a reconciliation account when posting a document, the account must be marked as 'reconciliation account ready for input' in the master, and that this condition is not met. The entered account is therefore not allowed.

**My question:**
With the exact same configuration, the alternative reconciliation account works correctly in F0859 (both G/L-based and PO-based invoices), but MIRO raises F5439 in every case. Why does MIRO behave differently from F0859 despite identical configuration? Is there an additional setting required specifically for MIRO, or does MIRO not support the alternative reconciliation account in Public Edition?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2602

Any guidance based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SE Import sccount assigned unknown
entry.content:
The import SE is a great efficiency gain option also ensuring consistency and reducing chance of human error.  It does not work for POs with account assignment unknown 

Why not? When will it? What alternative routes there are for these POs to import / mass process SEs?

entry.title: Request for Bulk Upload Facility in Post Outgoing Payment App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team 

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

We have identified that the Post Outgoing Payment app does not provide a bulk upload facility for processing multiple vendor payments.

Currently, users have to create outgoing payments manually one by one, which is time-consuming and increases the possibility of manual errors when processing a large number of vendor payments.

Could you please confirm the following:

  1. Is there any standard bulk upload functionality available for the Post Outgoing Payment app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. If available, please share the recommended approach or configuration.
  3. If this functionality is not currently supported, kindly confirm whether it is a standard product limitation and whether there are any planned roadmap enhancements.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: equest for Standard Process to Transfer Amount from G/L Account to Vendor Account upload options
entry.content:
Dear Team 

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

We have a business requirement to transfer an amount that is currently posted in a G/L account to a vendor account with upload options.

Could you please confirm the following:

  1. Is there any standard functionality or recommended process in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to transfer the balance from a G/L account to a vendor account?
  2. Which standard app or transaction should be used for this process?
  3. If this is not supported as a standard functionality, please confirm whether it is a product limitation and advise the recommended approach.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Automatic AlphaNumerical Serial and Equipment Number Generate
entry.content: We have a business requirement to generate Equipment numbers and Serial Numbers automatically in an alphanumeric format (for example, EQP000001 and SER000001) using internal number assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Please confirm the following: 1. Is automatic alphanumeric internal number assignment supported for Equipment and Serial Numbers? 2. If not, is there any standard configuration, SSCUI, CBC setting, or BAdI available to achieve this requirement? 3. Is this functionality planned in the SAP roadmap, or is SAP BTP or a side-by-side extension the only recommended approach? Thank you.

entry.title: Automatic Alphanumeric Internal Number Assignment for Equipment and Serial Number
entry.content:
We have a business requirement to generate Equipment numbers and Serial Numbers automatically in an alphanumeric format (for example, EQP000001 and SER000001) using internal number assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Please confirm the following:

1. Is automatic alphanumeric internal number assignment supported for Equipment and Serial Numbers?

2. If not, is there any standard configuration, SSCUI, CBC setting, or BAdI available to achieve this requirement?

3. Is this functionality planned in the SAP roadmap, or is SAP BTP or a side-by-side extension the only recommended approach?

Thank you.

entry.title: Clarification Required on BAdI FAA_DC_UPA_CUSTOMER in Depreciation Recalculation
entry.content:
Objective:

Our requirement is to modify the calculated depreciation amount for assets during the depreciation run executed using the Schedule Asset Accounting Jobs Fiori app. For this purpose, we have implemented the BAdI FAA_DC_UPA_CUSTOMER.

Issue Description:

While implementing the required logic, we attempted to debug the BAdI during the depreciation calculation/posting job. However, we observed that the depreciation amount available within the BAdI is always zero, even though the depreciation job is being executed successfully. As a result, we are unable to analyze or modify the calculated depreciation amount within the BAdI implementation.

Clarifications Required:

  • Is FAA_DC_UPA_CUSTOMER the appropriate BAdI to influence or modify the calculated depreciation amount for an asset during the depreciation run?
  • Is it possible to change the depreciation amount for an asset using this BAdI?
  •

entry.title: Change field selection in "Create Customer Project"
entry.content:
Dear Community user,

Background: In our project, there are hundreds of cost center in the system.
So, when our customer enter the Customer project or Internal project in the system, our customer wants to search Costcenter in the selection field.

Current Situation: The SAP Standard "Create Customer Project" does not provide search selection same as Project Manager or Customer field in the left side.
Also, When I put the costcenter code directly in the cost center field, the suggestion is not working.
Our customer is using Japanese language, english description search is working but Japanese description search is not working in current SAP verstion

Kindly share knowledge how to change this cost center field?

Best Regards,

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud: Inbound Delivery Printing (PDF)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I was wondering if there is a way to print or preview a PDF file for Inbound Delivery Documents like Sales Order and Purchase Order where there are an output and preview for Print Forms. When I try to select an Output Type, it says that "No possible entries exist for this field".

Regards,
Ley

entry.title: Deprecation of SOAP APIs for Asset Master Data and Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0563
entry.content:
I am reviewing the system changes outlined in the "Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608" on the SAP Help Portal.

Regarding the "Deprecation of SOAP APIs for Asset Master Data and Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0563":
It states that the SOAP APIs and communication scenario SAP_COM_0563 are being deprecated, but when exactly will they be decommissioned?

I am aware that deprecated apps remain valid for at least 12 months, as indicated on the page below; does this same rule apply to APIs?
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/5efa551420f24d738131134c8101168a.html?locale=en-US

We currently use these SOAP APIs for asset master data creation and need to make modifications; I would like to know the exact decommissioning date to plan our modification schedule.

entry.title: Can the requester specify/select an ad-hoc approver when creating a Supplier Invoice (Parked as Comp
entry.content:
*Title:** Can the requester specify/select an ad-hoc approver when creating a Supplier Invoice (Parked as Complete Workflow / WS00800303) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

We are implementing the Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices (Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete, WS00800303) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In the "Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices" app, we understand that approvers are determined by pre-configured rules (Role, Manager of Creator, Cost Center Responsible, specific User, or Team).

Our question: **Is it possible for the requester (the person creating the supplier invoice) to manually select/specify an approver at the time the invoice is created/submitted, similar to the "Ad-hoc Approver" functionality available for Purchase Orders in the "Manage Purchase Orders" app?**

Specifically:
1. Does the Supplier Invoice flexible workflow scenario (WS00800303 / WS00800251) support an ad-hoc approver step where the initiator can pick a user at runtime?
2. If not available out-of-the-box, is there a supported extension point (e.g., BAdI MRM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS) to let the invoice creator choose the approver dynamically?
3. Is this on SAP's roadmap, given that Ad-hoc Approver already exists for PO workflows?

Any confirmation from SAP or real-world configuration experience would be appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: MRP Run for FG Material Considers Supply and Demand from Other WBS Elements
entry.content:
Issue Description:

We are executing MRP for a Finished Goods (FG) material assigned to a specific WBS element.

Business Requirement:
When MRP is run for the FG material with a selected WBS, the system should generate procurement proposals (Purchase Requisitions and/or Production Orders) only for that WBS. The planning should be restricted to the selected WBS and should not consider supply or demand from other WBS elements.

Current Behavior:
After executing MRP for the FG material, the system considers dependent requirements, planned orders, and production orders belonging to other WBS elements while calculating the procurement proposals. As a result, the generated procurement proposals are influenced by other WBS elements instead of being planned independently for the selected WBS.

Expected Behavior:
The MRP run should be restricted to the selected WBS so that procurement proposals are generated exclusively for that WBS.

In SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud/On-Premise, this requirement can be achieved using transaction MD51.
